* It's a HUGE bike, and XXL 29er hardtail

bikes.description: This bike is a brand new 2016 model. It has a U-lock mount attached to its stem. It has a sticker below the drink rack that is from Wheel Base in Frederick, MD. Alpha Silver Aluminum material. On the underneath of the seat, there is a number of 328537. The drink rack is slightly broken off.
bikes.title: 2016 Trek 7.0 FX 500685
2015 Schwinn LL Bean Sport Trail 275 medium

bikes.description: Seat has a tear in the right side & padding is exposed. Bike had front and rear fenders. Bike had red LED Cygolite on rear seat post. Bike had cargo rack on back with a Domo Kun toy screwed into it. Front brake handle was damaged from a fall and slightly askew. Bike had a CR-MO sticker on frame, as well as a sticker from a bike shop in Racine, WI or Kenosha, WI.
bikes.title: 1999 GT Bicycles Outpost Trail

bikes.description:
Also missing a limited edition Brooks Cambium saddle.  

2012 Jamis Bosanova shadow white color, 54 cm frame; Brooks natural (tan rubber and cotton canvas) Cambium saddle, 899 stamped on the front rivet; Revolights;  Oakley Split Jacket cycling glasses with grey lens and transitions lens set; PDW payload rack;  Cat Eye wireless bike computer; Shimano SPD pedals (platform on one side, SPD clips on the other); 2 Frost River bike bags, Sawbill Trail bar bag and Echo Trail seat bag, tan color waxed canvas; Lazersport O2 helmet, black, with optional rear led light.

bikes.description: Has a broken right pedal.

bikes.description: Specialized M4 fully manipulated alloy frame, fully butted, Trail 29" geometry, externally relieved HT, SS compatible forged adjustable slider dropouts, replaceable der. hanger, disc only
bikes.title: 2011 Specialized Rockhopper Comp 29er

bikes.description: When we introduced our first cyclocross bike to America in 1986, few riders were even into cyclocross racing. Despite that, it didn't take long for cycling enthusiasts to figure out what our new Volpe excelled at; everything! Long distance road rides. Gravel road excursions. City commuting. Loaded touring. Rail trail adventures. Neighborhood errands. The Volpe checks off all the boxes! Short of criterium racing and technical single track, the Volpe handles all types of riding efficiently and comfortably. The Volpe melds the responsive handling of a road bike with the durability of a mountain bike. A variety of surfaces and conditions can be conquered thanks to greater tire clearance and grime-shedding cantilever brakes. Our exclusive Chromolite frame and steel fork provide a smooth ride and the strength to carry whatever supplies your adventure calls for. With eyelets for front and rear racks and a set of fenders, the Volpe is one of the most versatile models we offer. Frame: Bianchi Cr-Mo, triple butted main tubes, tig welded..
bikes.title: 2014 Bianchi Volpe

bikes.description: Alpha Red Aluminum w/R1i Trail Tuned Suspension, ABP pivot, Full Floater, Aluminum EVO Link, Hi/Lo chainstays, oversized sealed bearings, 120mm rear wheel travel
bikes.title: 2008 Trek Fuel EX 8

bikes.description: Specialized A1 Premium Aluminum, Trail 29" geometry, fully butted, formed TT, Double ORE DT, externally relieved HT, reinforced disc mount, forged dropouts w/ replaceable hanger, disc only
bikes.title: 2011 Specialized Hardrock Disc 29er

bikes.description: fenders front/back.  rear fender held on with black zip tie.  split orthopedic seat.  Trail-a-bike hitch on seat post.  1 front light and 2 rear lights.  Bike Gallery sticker.  New cabling and chain.  paint scratched/rust just below seat post.  Paint rubbed off to primer on one side of rear fork.  Bontrager puncture proof tires.  Small rip in handle bar wrap.  Stolen between Feb 4 and Feb 12.
bikes.title: 1986 Bridgestone 400

bikes.description: My 17.5" Frame, 26" Wheels, black and white, Specialized Hardrock Sport Disc was stolen from me on July 7, 2012. according to my own research and that done by the SCPD officers investigating my case, it has been resold three times since the night it was stolen. It has traveled from Downtown Santa Cruz, to another part of Santa Cruz, to the San Jose/Bay Area where the trail runs cold. This is apparently where it now resides. The buyer was a businessman in the San Jose area, the bike was sold for $475. I miss my bike and want it back. If anyone out there finds it or has it, please be my hero and return it to me. Here are the specifics of my case, prior to it's final known sale: At 7:00 pm on 7/7/2012, I parked and locked my black and white Specialized Hardrock Sport Disc in the alleyway behind Marini's Candy Shop and the Front Street Parking Garage in downtown Santa Cruz. I wove my Kryptonite lock through the bicycle rack, both wheels, and frame of my bicycle, thinking this would deter any thief from going after my bike. I went in to one of the local shops and grabbed dinner with friends. We came out around 8:00 pm and checked to see that my bike was still there. Since the alley was full of people going back and forth on their Saturday night,  and the bike appeared to be in tact, we decided it was safe to see a movie. When we returned to get my bike around 10:10 pm, it was gone, and so was the Kryptonite lock.There are minor scratches on the right side of the frame. There was an "Another Bike Shop" sticker on the rear bottom inside of the frame when I lost it. This may no longer be on the bike. The bike had a minor shifting problem in second gear, that resulted in a bit of a hard shift when changing speeds in this gear, and was most noticeable on the uphill. There are minor cosmetic defects that ensure I can identify the bike as mine if it is found. Keeping my hopes up and fingers crossed that somebody out there comes across it and helps me out. Thank you for reading this.   Specialized A1 Premium Aluminum, fully butted, formed TT, Double ORE DT, externally relieved HT, reinforced disc mount, forged dropouts w/ replaceable hanger, disc only
bikes.title: 2011 Specialized Hardrock Sport Disc

bikes.description: with Pinhead wheel locks and attachment for Trail-a-bike (which were likely removed); reflector on pedal missing and rubber cap on right side of handle bars. slightly thinner wheels than usual
bikes.title: 2011 Fuji Absolut

bikes.description: My women's Schwinn 700c Trail Way Hybrid was stolen between 7/15 at 10pm and 7/31 at 5pm. The rear relector has been removed so the seat will go down further. There is a head light bracket mounted on the handle bars. The rear light bracket is mounted below the seat post on the bike frame. There are small scratches on the side of the frame from leaning the bike on signs. There are black smudges on the frame below the seat from a U-lock. Otherwise the bike looks brand new. The Schwinn decal on the front still has plastic on it.
bikes.title: 2011 Schwinn unknown

bikes.description: Schwinn 700c Trail Way Hybrid, step through, 26" frame. Rear reflector has been removed so seat post will go down farther. Bracket for head light on handlebars. Brackets for rear light on frame instead of seat post. Nearly new, plastic still on Schwinn decal on front. Small scratches on side of frame from leaning against bike rack. Black smudges on top of frame from u-lock.
bikes.title: 2011 Schwinn 700c Trail Way Hybrid

bikes.description: Silver bike, blue kona logos, black truvative stem & handlebars, black odi lockon grips, xtr shifter brakes oldschool dull silver non-hydraulic, Kenda karma tires, burly black fork (never found brand) w/36mm stanchions & 20mm front hub (takes 12mm allen to remove)& "pedal damn it" sticker, Redline black cranks & black generic pedals, raceface chainrings x3, 8 speed rear, fox shock w/black spring, sealed bearings on upper suspension linkage (blue shields), Azonic Loveseat seat (all black kevlar), Kenda karma tires (square tread pattern) FRAME REWELDED AT RIGHT SIDE SUSPENPENSION PIVOT WHERE UPPER AND LOWER CHAINSTAYS MEET (PAINT BURNED AROUND AND BARE SILVER AT THE WELD). THIEVES ARE DANGEROUS AS THEY CLUBBED ME WITH BLACKJACK ON BACK OF THE HEAD JUST TO GET THE BIKE. CALL 911 IF YOU SEE IT.
bikes.title: 2002 Kona Bear Deelux

bikes.description: My bike, a magenta colored, fairly new, Fuji Absolute, was stolen from a bike lock-up a block North of the Burnside Bridge, parallel to the Eastern side of the Burnside Bridge Skate Park. The lock-up was near a building that looked like it was recently a business but is now abandoned and a movable chain fence. In front (to the East) there is a large empty field and behind are some bushes with a rough trail behind them leading down to a warehouse. My bike is has a grey milk cart attached to a black rack on the back of my bike with hose clamps. On the back of the milk crate there was a ripped and peeling bumper sticker that was green with white letters that read "Recycle Yourself" and had a picture of the recycling emblem. The larger part of the bumper sticker was ready to fall off and I'm not sure if it will still be on the bike or not. There was a black water bottle holder on the lower part of the bike and the chain showed slight signs of rust. Unfortunately, the reason it was stolen was because I had accidentally left my keys in the lock, which means the perpetrator probably still has the lock and key (if it is of any use I can supply you with the extra keys I have). The lock was a black lock with a thick, black, ridged, and bendable wire (for lack of a better term) that ended in a lock that the wire could insert itself into. The lock at the end was covered in black plastic that was slightly chipped at the bottom. A helmet was also with the bike. The helmet was a silver ski-style helmet, not usually used for biking, with yellow designs and various stickers on it. It had a black padded inside that was attached to a white Styrofoam piece that could be pulled out of the helmet. The keys taken with the bike had the bike on them, an old bike key and three other house keys.   Fuji Altair 2 aluminum with Tear Drop down tube, Double water bottle mounts. Rear triangle: Fuji Altair 2 custom tapered aluminum, Fuji forged road dropout with replaceable derailleur hanger
bikes.title: 2009 Fuji Absolute 2.0

bikes.description: 20 inch BMX black with a Maroon fork and the words FIT BIKE CO down the side
bikes.title: 2008 Fit bike Co. Team Trail

bikes.description: The bike is an Azonic edition, so it has lots of Azonic stickers and parts. It has a noticable golf ball sized dent on the drive train side, between the letter A and R (of the work Marin). If it has this dent, the bike was very likely stolen from me.
bikes.title: 2003 Marin Bikes Wildcat Trail
